name: Box MCP Server
what_it_does: Box MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Box — the enterprise cloud content management platform — uploading and downloading files, searching content, managing folders and permissions, and integrating Box's secure content management into agent-driven enterprise document workflows.
use_cases: Searching Box for relevant documents from enterprise knowledge agents
Uploading generated documents to Box from content creation agents
Reading Box files for analysis from document processing agents
Managing Box folder structures from organization agents
Sharing files and managing permissions from collaboration agents
Building enterprise document workflows with Box integration
not_for: Teams not using Box (use Google Drive or OneDrive MCPs instead)
Consumer file storage — Box is enterprise-focused
Teams needing specialized Box AI features (Box AI has separate APIs)
best_when: An enterprise team uses Box for secure content management and wants AI agents to read, write, and organize documents — integrating AI into Box-based document workflows.
avoid_when: Your team uses Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive — use the appropriate MCP for your storage platform.

auth.notes: Box OAuth 2.0 required. Create Box App in developer console. Client ID, client secret, and access token needed. Supports JWT (service account) or OAuth user flow.

pricing.notes: Box has free individual plan. Enterprise features require paid tier. Community MCP from box-community org — semi-official.

performance.rate_limits: Box API: 1,000 calls/minute per user token

known_agent_gotchas: OAuth app setup required in Box developer console — complex for simple use cases
JWT service account vs user OAuth — choose based on use case
Box API rate limits apply — 1,000 calls/minute per token
From box-community org — semi-official but not Box official
Enterprise Box features may require admin approval for app installation
File download/upload of large files needs chunked upload handling
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with scopes. From box-community org. Enterprise content — use JWT service account for automated workflows. Scope OAuth carefully.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. OAuth 2.0. Semi-official MCP. Enterprise content access — scope carefully.
